issue_title: defaults handling is case sensitive
issue_body:
Given:

```yaml
defaults:
  request_headers:
      Foo-Bar: value

tests:
- name: some test
   GET: /
   request_headers:
     foo-bar: value
```

`foo-bar` will not clobber `Foo-Bar`.

This may or may not be a bug. I initially thought it was (because I got caught by it) but while writing this I realized there's no good way to make it guess well. There are likely times where case does matter in a default (consider all defaults here, not jus request headers).

If we say it is case sensitive and stick with it then it is explicit and predictable.

/cc @FND

issue_body:

[16.fa.gz](https://github.com/lh3/minimap2/files/1221426/16.fa.gz)

doing an all-vs-all mapping of these sequences (1.38Mb total) takes about 20 seconds:

```
$ time ~/install/minimap2/minimap2 -X -x ava-ont 16.fa 16.fa > /dev/null

issue_title: Leading zeroes in floating-point indexes raise a TomlError
issue_body:
The following code raises a TomlError exception both under Python2 and Python3:

`````python
import pytoml
pytoml.loads("""
# This is a full-line comment
maximum_error = 4.85e-06 # 1 arcsec
""")
`````

The error message is the following:
`````
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 4, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pytoml/parser.py", line 23, in loads
    ast = _p_toml(src)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pytoml/parser.py", line 344, in _p_toml
    s.expect_eof()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pytoml/parser.py", line 124, in expect_eof
    return self._expect(self.consume_eof())
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pytoml/parser.py", line 164, in _expect
    raise TomlError('msg', self._pos[0], self._pos[1], self._filename)
pytoml.core.TomlError: <string>(3, 24): msg
`````

Removing the leading zero solves the problem:

`````python
import pytoml
pytoml.loads("""
# This is a full-line comment
maximum_error = 4.85e-6 # 1 arcsec
""")
# Result: {'maximum_error': 4.85e-06}
`````

issue_title: Cannot get correct zip URL of ugoira
issue_body:
If I tried to get info of an ugoira:

~~~Python
json_result = api.works(60346068)
~~~

`json_result.response[0].metadata.zip_urls.ugoira600x600` will contain an URL which link to an ZIP file. 

For example, http://i1.pixiv.net/img-zip-ugoira/img/2016/12/12/13/17/20/60346068_ugoira600x600.zip.

But this file can not be open or unzip.

If I type `pixiv.context.ugokuIllustFullscreenData.src` in web browser console, or look at HTML source code, I will get an URL link to another ZIP file, which contains all frames of the work.

The URL of right ZIP file in my example is http://i1.pixiv.net/img-zip-ugoira/img/2016/12/12/13/17/20/60346068_ugoira1920x1080.zip

It seems we can get the right URL by changing `...ugoira600x600...` to `...ugoira1920x1080...`. But I wonder if there are some more parameters should be added when fetching work information?

issue_title: Fetching BibTeX meta is not clear in the doc
issue_body:
Hi,

According to the doc, I can think of two ways to get a BibTeX metadata for a given ISBN:

* Either `meta(isbn)` and formatting it.
* Either `doi2tex(doi(isbn))`.

The second method seems to be more reliable, in particular as it relies on the DOI system, which is using a unique identifier and gives better results than `meta` in my experience.

However, taken from [https://www.doi.org/factsheets/ISBN-A.html](the ISBN-A page on doi.org), they say:

> ISBN-As do not automatically exist for every ISBN; they exist only once the agency has registered them in the DOI System.

Whereas when looking at [your code](https://github.com/xlcnd/isbnlib/blob/master/isbnlib/_ext.py#L47) you always derive an ISBN-A from a given ISBN13.

For my use case, I would need a way to check whether the generated ISBN-A is indeed a valid ISBN-A linked to any DOI or not. And reading the doc and looking a bit at the code, I was not sure what is the expected behavior of `doi2tex(doi(isbn))` when the ISBN-A does not exist.

Thanks a lot!

P.S.: As I do not have examples of ISBNs without associated ISBN-A, I cannot check this easily =( Sorry if this is already documented somewhere else.

issue_title: Does green support different file naming patterns?
issue_body:
At various points in `loader.py`, it looks like green intends to support the idea of a `file_pattern`. But I don't see any command-line options to control this behavior. Also, I don't think that the value of `file_pattern` is actually being passed through the call stack down to the underlying `discover()` function. Perhaps I'm overlooking something.

Anyway, green looks like a great project and I'm eager to try it out (frustrated with nose, pytest, and unittest). But on my team we follow a `*_tests.py` naming convention, so I've been unable to get out of the starting gate.

issue_title: Exclude certain URLs
issue_body:
Hey *,

is there a way to exclude a URL from the alerts or mark it as a false positive?

What I've done so far is:
- `zap-cli exclude "$URL"` ( where $URL is the complete url )
- `zap-cli exclude "$REG"` ( where $REG is a regex that matches the exact payload )
- `zap-cli quick-scan -s xss -r -e "$URL" $APP`
- `zap-cli quick-scan -s xss - r -e "$REG" $APP`

unfortunately, none of those seems to work.
I can't provide more details on which url I'm scanning since it is work related, but the payload that zap-cli outputs is: `?query=%3Balert%281%29%3B` which decoded is `?query=;alert(1);`

Cheers,
Marvin

issue_title: Zap-cli not reporting results of passive scan
issue_body:
Zap-cli has been showing some odd behavior when I to view the results thus far of a passive scan. I set zap-cli to passive scan on url 127.0.0.1 and port 8080, and then had firefox proxy through this but when I run zap-cli zap-cli alerts -l Low -f json it returns empty brackets. When I run zap-cli report -o report.xml it returns a file like this: 

``<?xml version="1.0"?><OWASPZAPReport version="2.7.0" generated="Wed, 6 Jun 2018 17:59:43">
<site name="http://example.com" host="example.com" port="80" ssl="false"><alerts></alerts></site><site name="http://ocsp.digicert.com" host="ocsp.digicert.com" port="80" ssl="false"><alerts></alerts></site><site name="http://pagead2.googlesyndication.com" host="pagead2.googlesyndication.com" port="80" ssl="false"><alerts></alerts></site><site name="http://platform.twitter.com" host="platform.twitter.com" port="80" ssl="false"><alerts></alerts></site><site name="http://m.addthisedge.com" host="m.addthisedge.com" port="80" ssl="false"><alerts></alerts></site><site name="http://m.addthis.com" host="m.addthis.com" port="80" ssl="false"><alerts></alerts></site><site name="http://randomcolour.com" host="randomcolour.com" port="80" ssl="false"><alerts></alerts></site><site name="http://www.theuselessweb.com" host="www.theuselessweb.com" port="80" ssl="false"><alerts></alerts></site><site name="http://s7.addthis.com" host="s7.addthis.com" port="80" ssl="false"><alerts></alerts></site><site name="http://www.ismycomputeron.com" host="www.ismycomputeron.com" port="80" ssl="false"><alerts></alerts></site><site name="http://redscientist.com" host="redscientist.com" port="80" ssl="false"><alerts></alerts></site></OWASPZAPReport>`` 

All the websites I've been visiting are listed, but no vulnerabilities.

What's even more odd is that I've tried troubleshooting this and I've gotten the correct output from alerts but only after a significant amount of time. In one case I used the alerts and reports function (but nothing else) a few times for a half an hour and they weren't returning what I wanted them to. I then left my computer for another half an hour and came back and then ran the alerts command I mentioned earlier. It gave the passive scan results I wanted on all the websites I had visited in the hour I had it open the first time I ran it. The data for the vulnerabilities is definitely there but something seems to be holding it up, and I'm not sure what it is or how I'm eventually getting the report I want other than waiting long enough. Active scan and quick scan results are added to the report immediately, but passive scan results seem to be held up by something or sometimes not display at all.

issue_title: Fraud Detection Example not working as expected
issue_body:
Hi,

I am trying to run fraud detection example and using following code snippet 

```
from durable.lang import *
with ruleset('fraud_detection'):

    @when_all(c.first << m.amount > 100,
              c.second << m.amount > c.first.amount * 2,
              c.third << m.amount > (c.first.amount + c.second.amount) / 2)
    def detected(c): 
        print('fraud detected -> {0} {1}'.format(c.first.amount, c.first.id))
        print('               -> {0} {1}'.format(c.second.amount, c.second.id))
        print('               -> {0} {1}'.format(c.third.amount, c.third.id))

    @when_start
    def start(host):
        sid = 18
        host.post('fraud_detection', {'id': 30, 'sid': sid, 'amount': 120})
        host.post('fraud_detection', {'id': 50, 'sid': sid, 'amount': 1000})
        
run_all()

```

Ideally since I am only posting only 2 events, fraud_detection ruleset should not complete. However I am getting following output on running above code 

```
fraud detected -> 120 30
               -> 1000 50
               -> 1000 50
```

Can someone explain why this is happening ? Am I missing something here ? 

**Note**: I cleared redis database before running this so that test starts afresh.

issue_title: Cleanco not properly identifying Czech companies
issue_body:
Hello, thank you for your work on cleanco.

Cleanco does not seem to work properly for czech companies, eg:

```
>>> c = cleanco("Company s.r.o.")
>>> c.type() is None
True
>>> c.country() is None
True
>>> c = cleanco("Company a.s.")
>>> c.type() is None
True
>>> c.country() is None
True
```

Although I see that `'s.r.o.'` and `'a.s.'` are present in termdata.py in the right places.

One more detail, there is also the possibility to use `'spol. s r.o.'` instead of `'s.r.o.'` in Czech Republic - they are equivalent. `'spol. s r.o.'` is not present in termdata.py.

issue_title: probleme with adding extensions into gpx
issue_body:
Hello
First thank you very much for the development of gpxpy, it helps really to work with gpx in my project. 
Actually I'm trying to some custom extension into an generated gpx file. But the extension doesn't appear into the generated file, although python doesn't give an error message. 
To test this bug, I've wrote a little programm which takes trackpoints from one file (test_import.gpx) and copy them to another (export.gpx) while trying to maintain the extensions of the original file. 

```
import gpxpy
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

gpx_file = open('test_import.gpx', 'r')
read_file = gpxpy.parse(gpx_file)

gpx = gpxpy.gpx.GPX()

gpx.extensions=read_file.extensions
gpx.nsmap=read_file.nsmap

for track in read_file.tracks:
    gpx_track = gpxpy.gpx.GPXTrack()
    #gpx_track.extensions = track.extensions
    #does work, give both extensions back

    gpx_track.extensions = track.extensions[1]
    #should in theory only add the first extension into the new file, but doesn't add one

    #gpx_track.extensions =  ET.fromstring('<locus:activity xmlns:locus="http://www.locusmap.eu">hiking</locus:activity>\n\t')
    #should in theory add the second extension into the new gpx file, but doesn't add one.
    for segment in track.segments:
        gpx_segment = gpxpy.gpx.GPXTrackSegment()

        for point in segment.points:
            gpx_segment.points.append(gpxpy.gpx.GPXTrackPoint(point.latitude, point.longitude, point.elevation))

        gpx_track.segments.append(gpx_segment)

    gpx.tracks.append(gpx_track)

with open("export.gpx", 'w', encoding="utf-8") as file:
    file.write(gpx.to_xml("1.1"))
```

The extensions used in the import file are:
```
<gpx_style:line>
	<gpx_style:color>0000FF</gpx_style:color>
	<gpx_style:opacity>0.59</gpx_style:opacity>
	<gpx_style:width>6.0</gpx_style:width>
</gpx_style:line>
<locus:activity>hiking</locus:activity>
```
After a little bit of testing I've found that the first extension (gpx:style) is allways copied to the new file, but the other one I need in my project (locus:activity) isn't copied, even if I try to copy also the namespace. Additionally I've tryed also to generate this extension manually (line with Et.fromstring), cause in my project I need to generate this extension specific to track which is generated. Unfortanetely I've failed here too, in the generated gpx file I don't find the locus:activity extension. 
The used version of python is 3.6.5, operating system is Windows 10, the used version of the gpxpy libary is the one from the dev branche, I've downloaded it one week ago. 
So maybe it is bug or maybe it is entirely my fault, cause I'm a beginner in python. Thank you very much for any help. 



[used_files.zip](https://github.com/tkrajina/gpxpy/files/2186266/used_files.zip)

issue_body:
Is there any plans to have pretty outputs (table or at least formatted) for the ```list``` operation on the CLI?

Right now, the CLI assumes a verbosity of DEBUG level, and outputs the Python representation of the result (managed by the provider). If --log_level=ERROR is used, no output is generated, which defeats the CLI usage, in my opinion.

Is this behavior expected? Would you be open to a PR for that?

issue_title: don't separate library names in requirements.txt
issue_body:
When running with double aggressive on requirements.txt
the name of libraries with a hyphen get corrupted with spaces 
which leads to errors when feeding the requirments file to pip. 

Version: 
autopep8 1.3.5 (pycodestyle: 2.3.1)


Expected output: 

should not separate hyphenated library names with spaces. 


Current output: 

hyphenated library names get separated with spaces 

Example: 


make a file requirements.txt with library names containing hyphen 
run autopep8 --aggressive --aggressive 
```
$cat requirements.txt
more-itertools
psycopg2-binary
pytest-cov
python-dateutil
$autopep8 --in-place --aggressive --aggressive requirements.txt
$cat requirements.txt
more - itertools
psycopg2 - binary
pytest - cov
python - dateutil
```

issue_title: Issue with motd on Cisco IOS
ValueError: Router prompt not found: User: in Cisco WLC
issue_body:
Hello Kirk,

I recently ran into the same problem as a previous [bug](https://github.com/ktbyers/netmiko/issues/558). I have tried adding a global delay factor but it was unable to properly realize there was actually a 'NetMikoAuthenticationException'. I noticed a previous user on that thread had said it was the way Cisco WLC handles Auth which I guess can be true but realized that it really only failed on bad password. When some cisco_wlc devices fail auth, they request 'User:' again, which to netmiko, seems like a valid response. 

For the time being I have updated our cisco_wlc_ssh.py since we only have 1 type of cisco_wlc at my company. 
```
    def session_preparation(self):
        '''
        Prepare the session after the connection has been established

        Cisco WLC uses "config paging disable" to disable paging
        '''
        self._test_channel_read()
        try:
            self.set_base_prompt()
        except Exception as e:
            if 'User:' in e.message:
                msg = "Authentication failure: unable to connect {device_type} {ip}:{port}".format(
                    device_type=self.device_type, ip=self.host, port=self.port)
                raise NetMikoAuthenticationException(msg)
        self.disable_paging(command="config paging disable")
        # Clear the read buffer
        time.sleep(.3 * self.global_delay_factor)
        self.clear_buffer()
```

I attempted to modify BaseConnection to add self.repeat_response as an empty list and appending output of _read_channel(self) using the self.repeat_response += output.split() below then parsing within cisco_wlc_ssh.py specifically to its session_preparation to see if a response occurs twice meaning it received back what it started with (i hope i dont sound like a madman). 
```
======== base_connection.py ========
class BaseConnection(object):
        <omitted pre-code>
        self.repeat_response = []
    def _read_channel(self):
        """Generic handler that will read all the data from an SSH or telnet channel."""
        if self.protocol == 'ssh':
            output = ""
            while True:
                if self.remote_conn.recv_ready():
                    outbuf = self.remote_conn.recv(MAX_BUFFER)
                    if len(outbuf) == 0:
                        raise EOFError("Channel stream closed by remote device.")
                    output += outbuf.decode('utf-8', 'ignore')
                    self.repeat_response += output.split()
                else:
                    break
        elif self.protocol == 'telnet':
            output = self.remote_conn.read_very_eager().decode('utf-8', 'ignore')
        elif self.protocol == 'serial':
            output = ""
            while (self.remote_conn.in_waiting > 0):
                output += self.remote_conn.read(self.remote_conn.in_waiting)
        log.debug("read_channel: {}".format(output))
        return output

======== cisco_wlc_ssh.py ========
class CiscoWlcSSH(BaseConnection):
    <omitted code>
    def session_preparation(self):
        '''
        Prepare the session after the connection has been established

        Cisco WLC uses "config paging disable" to disable paging
        '''
        self._test_channel_read()
        if [a for a in self.repeat_response if (self.repeat_response.count(a)==2) and ('>' not in self.repeat_response)]:
            msg = "Authentication failure: unable to connect {device_type} {ip}:{port}".format(
                device_type=self.device_type, ip=self.host, port=self.port)
            raise NetMikoAuthenticationException(msg)
        self.set_base_prompt()
        self.disable_paging(command="config paging disable")
        # Clear the read buffer
        time.sleep(.3 * self.global_delay_factor)
        self.clear_buffer()
```
That way, hopefully, you aren't affecting too much, other than cisco_wlc devices but I may be totally off base. Just hoping to help and learning how to work in a github community on a project. Any feedback would be great.

Thanks!
Gabe

spot_cache = cacheutils.LRU(max_size=20000, on_miss=get_spot)

When I try to access the cache with

spot_cache.get('some-cache-key')

issue_title: Path.copy_stat ignores times and perms args
issue_body: Path.copy_stat always copies both mtime/atime and mode, regardless of the `times` and `perms` args it is passed. Given the implementation of Path.copy, this means only times=True,perms=True and times=False,perms=False work properly. Passing True,False or False,True results in both times and perms being copied.

issue_title: No definitions found for certain scenario
issue_body:
Please see a detailed description and discussion in tkf/emacs-jedi#281, which turns out to be probably a Jedi issue. In brief:

1. Clone [this repository](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr): `git clone https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr`
2. Go to [line 49](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr/blob/master/aslam_offline_calibration/kalibr/python/kalibr_imu_camera_calibration/IccCalibrator.py#L49) and try to find definitions of `addDesignVariables`. No definitions are found, yet the `addDesignVariables` function is defined in [IccSensors.py](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr/blob/master/aslam_offline_calibration/kalibr/python/kalibr_imu_camera_calibration/IccSensors.py#L316), which is a python file in the same directory.

Here is a Python console session showing the fault (courtesy [syohex](https://github.com/syohex) - which shows that I'm not the only one affected):
```
% ipython
Python 2.7.10 (default, Oct 23 2015, 19:19:21)
Type "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.

IPython 5.1.0 -- An enhanced Interactive Python.
?         -> Introduction and overview of IPython's features.
%quickref -> Quick reference.
help      -> Python's own help system.
object?   -> Details about 'object', use 'object??' for extra details.

In [1]: import jedi

In [2]: source = open('IccCalibrator.py', 'r').read()

In [3]: script = jedi.Script(source, 49, len('        self.CameraChain.'), 'IccCalibrator.py')

In [4]: defs = script.goto_definitions()

In [5]: defs
Out[5]: []
```

I am running Ubuntu 14.04 and:
```
$ pip show jedi
---
Name: jedi
Version: 0.10.0
Location: /usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages
Requires: 
$ pip show python
---
Name: Python
Version: 2.7
Location: /usr/lib/python2.7/lib-dynload
Requires: 
```

Since [line 177](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr/blob/master/aslam_offline_calibration/kalibr/python/kalibr_calibrate_imu_camera#L177) calls the [registerCamChain](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr/blob/master/aslam_offline_calibration/kalibr/python/kalibr_imu_camera_calibration/IccCalibrator.py#L58) function with `camChain` (an `IccCameraChain` object) argument, Jedi should make the guess that `self.CameraChain` is an `IccCameraChain` object and thus should be able to find the definition of `addDesignVariables` on [line 49](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr/blob/master/aslam_offline_calibration/kalibr/python/kalibr_imu_camera_calibration/IccCalibrator.py#L49), which is a method of the `IccCameraChain` class.

issue_title: MANIFEST.in wisely include only revisioned files
issue_body:
MANIFEST.in controls what files will end up in the source tar.gz distribution. Right now MANIFEST.in is a static file edited manually which doesn't take into account about whether files are added, removed or moved via GIT. It also doesn't take into account files which may be in the working dir but are not under revision control. As such it'd be better to have a dynamic `make generate-manifest` command which uses `git ls-files` to get a list of all files under revision control and writes them in MANIFEST.in.
This way we'll be certain the tar.gz distribution has no extraneous or missing files.

issue_title: Add option to xso.Child descriptor for strict type checking and use it for PubSub Items and IQs
issue_body:
It is annoying that currently, if you simply forget to designate your class as payload for IQ stanzas or PubSub Items, you are able to *send* data, but from the application perspective you only get an exception (IQ stanzas) or empty item (PubSub) back.

It would be better if that would fail early, e.g. at assignment time to that descriptor. We should make the typechecking optional, because ``isinstance`` is rather expensive and in most of the cases, XSOs are an implementation detail. This is obviously different for IQ stanzas and PubSub Items, where a friendlier (in the sense of failing loud and early when you make a mistake) interface is appropriate.

issue_title: Creating a NetNS does not work on i386 architecture (Ubuntu, at least)
issue_body:
Ran into this problem while testing for issue #211.

Test script:

    #!/usr/bin/env python3

    from pyroute2 import IPDB
    from pyroute2 import netns
    from pyroute2 import NetNS

    print('creating netns')
    nl = NetNS('test')

    print('creating netns ipdb')
    ipdb = IPDB(nl=nl)

    print('creating gre')
    testgre0 = ipdb.create(ifname='testgre0', kind='gre', gre_local='192.0.2.0', gre_remote='192.0.2.1') if 'testgre0' not in ipdb.by_name.keys() else ipdb.interfaces['testgre0']
    print('committing')
    ipdb.commit()
    print('bringing up gre')
    testgre0.up()
    print('committing')
    ipdb.commit()

    print('bringing down gre')
    testgre0.down()
    print('committing')
    ipdb.commit()
    print('removing gre')
    testgre0.remove()
    print('committing')
    ipdb.commit()

    print('removing netns')
    nl.remove()
    print('releasing netns ipdb')
    ipdb.release()

Output on a 64-bit Ubuntu VM:

    root@ubuntu:~# python3 test.py
    creating netns
    creating netns ipdb
    creating gre
    committing
    bringing up gre
    committing
    bringing down gre
    committing
    removing gre
    committing
    removing netns
    releasing netns ipdb

Output on a 32-bit Ubuntu VM:

    root@ubuntu:~# python3 test.py
    creating netns
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "test.py", line 8, in <module>
        nl = NetNS('test')
      File "/usr/lib/python3.4/pyroute2/__init__.py", line 90, in __new__
        return cls.registry['class'](*argv, **kwarg)
      File "/usr/lib/python3.4/pyroute2/netns/nslink.py", line 190, in __init__
        super(NetNS, self).__init__()
      File "/usr/lib/python3.4/pyroute2/netlink/nlsocket.py", line 279, in __init__
        super(NetlinkMixin, self).__init__()
      File "/usr/lib/python3.4/pyroute2/remote/__init__.py", line 226, in __init__
        raise init['error']
    OSError: [Errno 22] failed to open netns: 'test'
    ERROR:root:forced shutdown procedure, clean up netns manually

Despite the error shown above, the network namespace actually gets created. Creating network namespaces manually using `ip netns add test` works fine. Using Ubuntu 14.04 LTS with kernel 4.2.0-27-generic on both 32-bit and 64-bit.

issue_title: Exception handling for KeyboardInterrupt does nothing
issue_body:
While looking into why the bot doesn't exit after one SIGINT, I noticed the exception handling in `Client.run` introduced by 6d26d73. It looks like the code was taken from http://stackoverflow.com/a/27910822/385891 . The main issue is that it calls `loop.run_forever` again for some reason.

It also calls `Future.exception` but ignores the return value. https://docs.python.org/3/library/asyncio-task.html#asyncio.Future.exception doesn't raise, but even if it did, you have an `except: pass` right after that would drop it.

Possibly related: https://github.com/Rapptz/discord.py/pull/8

issue_title: Subclassing existing ROM models
issue_body:
Are there any hidden pitfalls to creating a subclass to an existing ROM model?

Use case for me is I'd like to store simulation data in a separate database, so that it can imitate the structure exactly but avoid conflicts with live data. E.G.:

```python
class Data(rom.Model):
    id = rom.PrimaryKey(indexed=True)
    data = rom.Integer()

class SimulatedData(Data):
    _conn = get_simulation_db()
```

Metaclass mechanics are a bit fuzzy to me, and the lack of explicit mention of deeper subclasses in the _ModelMetaclass makes me a little nervous. Even though the above code snippet runs without issue, I'd like to make sure there aren't any obvious traps I'm walking into.

issue_title: How to filter by column != None?
issue_body:

I tried doing: `Model.query.filter(Model.many21=(0,None))` which gave the right results.

If this type of query is possible, I'd suggest a constant in rom module like "rom.NotNull" or "rom.NotNone" or "rom.IS_DEFINED" and documenting clearly.

issue_title: .local resolution of local machine on McOS Sierra is disabled.
issue_body:
.local resolution of local machine on Sierra is disabled. This means that commands like rs.initiate() on a local host fails.

Currently a workaround I found is to activate any of the filesharing options on the mac. However, this is should not be the formal way to address this as this is a security risk.

issue_title: JSON to modify an idf file
issue_body:
### Background 

This functionality comes from a problem that we had.

- We were running E+ using eppy on a remote server
- the remote server could be triggered by http requests. To start a run or to retrieve results
- for each run, we had to make small modifications to the idf file

The question here was:
- in what format/syntax should the idf changes be sent to the server
- how are the changes made on the remote idf file

The solution to this was to use json to send the changes. 
Eppy was modified to be able use the json to make changes to the idf file
Most of this has been coded and documented (in the develop branch)

I'll put some of the documentation in this thread soon and then close the issue.

issue_title: Replicate a slide in version 0.6.1
issue_body:
I was able to use a workaround code provided by robintw and zhong2000 to duplicate slides
(see issue #132 and #232)

The workaround works well with version 0.5.8. However, it no longer works with 0.6.1
The error is associated with the line: for key, value in six.iteritems(source.rels):
AttributeError: 'Slide' object has no attribute 'rels'

rels is no longer an attribute for a Slide object, any suggestion on how to patch this code in order to make it work under 0.6.1?

Thanks!

Test code is attached below:
```
import pptx
import copy
import six

def _get_blank_slide_layout(pres):
     layout_items_count = [len(layout.placeholders) for layout in pres.slide_layouts]
     min_items = min(layout_items_count)
     blank_layout_id = layout_items_count.index(min_items)
     return pres.slide_layouts[blank_layout_id]

def copy_slide(pres,pres1,index):
     source = pres.slides[index]
     blank_slide_layout = _get_blank_slide_layout(pres)
     dest = pres1.slides.add_slide(blank_slide_layout)

     for shp in source.shapes:
          el = shp.element
          newel = copy.deepcopy(el)
          dest.shapes._spTree.insert_element_before(newel, 'p:extLst')

          for key, value in six.iteritems(source.rels):
                     # Make sure we don't copy a notesSlide relation as that won't exist
                   if not "notesSlide" in value.reltype:
                           dest.rels.add_relationship(value.reltype, value._target, value.rId)
          return dest
from pptx import Presentation
SLD_LAYOUT_TITLE_AND_CONTENT = 1
prs = Presentation()
slide_layout = prs.slide_layouts[SLD_LAYOUT_TITLE_AND_CONTENT]
slide = prs.slides.add_slide(slide_layout)
copy_slide(prs, prs, 0)


```

issue_title: Misleading behavior when using multiple data feeds
issue_body:
### Scenario

I have a 10 minute strategy that uses 1 minute indicators.

### Minimal Code to Illustrate the Problem

```python
import io, datetime

import backtrader as bt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

class DummyStrategy(bt.Strategy):
    def __init__(self):
        self.clock = False

    def notify_trade(self, trade):
        print("{} Trade opened at {}".format(trade.open_datetime(),  trade.price))

    def next(self):
        print(
            self.data0.datetime.time(),
            self.data0.open[0], self.data0.high[0], self.data0.low[0], self.data0.close[0],
            '/',
            self.data1.datetime.time(),
            self.data1.open[0], self.data1.high[0], self.data1.low[0], self.data1.close[0])

        if self.data0.datetime.time() == datetime.time(9, 30) and not self.bought:
            self.buy()
            self.bought = True

def getdata():
    data = io.StringIO(
"""time,open,high,low,close
2016-11-01 09:20:00,22959,22969,22954,22963
2016-11-01 09:21:00,22963,22965,22946,22959

2016-11-01 09:31:00,23051,23083,23051,23061
2016-11-01 09:32:00,23061,23090,23060,23077
2016-11-01 09:33:00,23078,23085,23070,23074
2016-11-01 09:34:00,23074,23083,23063,23070
2016-11-01 09:35:00,23070,23071,23051,23052
2016-11-01 09:36:00,23051,23055,23039,23055
2016-11-01 09:37:00,23055,23068,23048,23065

2016-11-01 09:39:00,23052,23081,23042,23081
2016-11-01 09:40:00,23081,23091,23044,23045
""")

    data.seek(0)
    return data

if __name__ == '__main__':
    c = bt.Cerebro(stdstats=False)

    df = pd.read_csv(getdata(), parse_dates=['time'], index_col='time')

    feed = bt.feeds.PandasData(dataname=df, openinterest=None, volume=None)

    c.adddata(feed)

    c.broker.setcash(1000000)
    c.run()
```

### The Output

```
09:20:00 22959.0 22969.0 22954.0 22963.0 / 09:20:00 22959.0 22969.0 22954.0 22963.0
09:20:00 22959.0 22969.0 22954.0 22963.0 / 09:21:00 22963.0 22965.0 22946.0 22959.0

09:20:00 22959.0 22969.0 22954.0 22963.0 / 09:25:00 22958.0 22994.0 22956.0 22992.0
09:20:00 22959.0 22969.0 22954.0 22963.0 / 09:26:00 22992.0 23007.0 22987.0 22994.0
09:20:00 22959.0 22969.0 22954.0 22963.0 / 09:27:00 22993.0 23024.0 22992.0 23024.0
09:20:00 22959.0 22969.0 22954.0 22963.0 / 09:28:00 23024.0 23100.0 23024.0 23088.0
09:20:00 22959.0 22969.0 22954.0 22963.0 / 09:29:00 23088.0 23095.0 23064.0 23072.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:30:00 23074.0 23078.0 23047.0 23049.0
2016-11-01 09:30:00 Trade opened at 22963.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:31:00 23051.0 23083.0 23051.0 23061.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:32:00 23061.0 23090.0 23060.0 23077.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:33:00 23078.0 23085.0 23070.0 23074.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:34:00 23074.0 23083.0 23063.0 23070.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:35:00 23070.0 23071.0 23051.0 23052.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:36:00 23051.0 23055.0 23039.0 23055.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:37:00 23055.0 23068.0 23048.0 23065.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:38:00 23067.0 23072.0 23048.0 23051.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:39:00 23052.0 23081.0 23042.0 23081.0
09:40:00 23051.0 23091.0 23039.0 23045.0 / 09:40:00 23081.0 23091.0 23044.0 23045.0
```

### The Problem

`backtrader` gives me the price of `22963`. However, looking at the 1-minute data (on the right), it's obvious that I'll never get filled at that good price in real life:

```
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:30:00 23074.0 23078.0 23047.0 23049.0
2016-11-01 09:30:00 Trade opened at 22963.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:31:00 23051.0 23083.0 23051.0 23061.0
09:30:00 22963.0 23100.0 22946.0 23049.0 / 09:32:00 23061.0 23090.0 23060.0 23077.0
```

So, after some debugging, the problem here is that I added the larger timeframe before the smaller timeframe, and `self.buy()` is buying into the bigger timeframe. For this particular purpose, the problem can be fixed by adding the smaller timerframe first according to the documentation.

However, the behavior here is totally counter-intuitive. I cannot think of any case where filling the order with `22963` ever makes sense. This seems to be completely a side effect rather than an intention. Even if I am buying the 10-minute timeframe, the correct behavior should be filling the order at `09:40` with price `23051`.

The existing behavior is actually very misleading and dangerous, because this basically allows the strategy to travel back through time and obtain a good price. The whole point of the negative indexing on data is to prevent accidental data-snooping. And I feel this does introduce it.

As a contrived example, what if I trade instrument A with 10 minute bar, using indicators of 1 minute data in instrument B? In that case, even if I add the smaller timeframe instrument B first, I'd still have to call `self.buy(data1)` and have no way to escape this bug except some heavy hacks.

### Proposed Solution

In `BackBroker.next`, maintain an internal clock for each data feed and disallow "travel back in time" in `_try_exec`

issue_title: Command line completion support
issue_body:
I've been looking into supporting something like [argcomplete](https://argcomplete.readthedocs.org/en/latest/) for Plumbum. There's a lot of work to do, but due to clean design of `plumbum.cli`, it looks like it's not that hard. I have an early test at https://github.com/henryiii/plumbum/tree/argcomp , just a proof of concept at the moment, but it does support argcomlete style completion with `-` and `--` names. It does not use argcomplete, but it assumes a completion system like argcomplete has been installed. I'm planning to contact the author of argcomplete to see if I can get permission to use identical names, etc, since it would be very nice if these packages didn't conflict (it's hard to add more than one completion extender, at least it was when I was working with argcomplete a few years ago, so that's important).

The system works like this: if you include the `#PYTHON_ARGCOMPLETE_OK` comment at the top, the global completer tries to run the program with a few special flags and file descriptors set. `plumbum.cli` recognises those flags and offers completions in the same style as true argcomplete functions (without all the monkey patching!). Current code does not need changing (save for the adding the flag above).

If you checkout the referenced branch, make sure the global argcomplete script is activated, then try `SimpleApp.py` in `/tests`, it should offer completions when tab is pressed after typing one or two dashes.

issue_title: Provide a "fixed seed" setting
issue_body:
Verifier supports a configurable random number generator and all use of randomness goes through that.

However if you do not provide one it will just create one with the default seed. It is desirable to run tests in a deterministic mode, which you could do by having Verifier create its random with a fixed seed instead.

In this case it would be better to have the randomisation happening *per falsify run* rather than be on the Verifier object.

Planned solution is to create the seed as some sort of hash of the hypothesis. e.g. use hypothesis.__name__ or inspect.getsource(hypothesis) if hypothesis is a lambda. This ensures tests cannot interfere with each others' randomness.

issue_title: Streaming or sequence widget
issue_body:
A loosely defined idea for now, to be expanded on later. Many variations can be imagined, some specific use cases would be needed to elaborate on details.

This widget would represent a sequence of data widgets,  but could present itself as a single data widget to an external widget that uses it. The idea is to store (cache) a number of snapshots among which the current one can be selected by a time variable.

Setting time between snapshot times could optionally lead to interpolation between data widget arrays.

Implementing this widget well could solve playback and replay of time series with minimal effort in libraries using ipydatawidgets.

issue_title: JupyterThemes does not allow changing notebooks filename inside of notebook
Use with pyinstaller frozen exe results in errors on Windows
issue_body:
Using this lib (for either ffprobe or ffmpeg) results in the following error when running on Windows

`[WinError 6] The handle is invalid`

Applying the suggested workaround from https://github.com/pyinstaller/pyinstaller/wiki/Recipe-subprocess to always set stdin resolves the issue for me

i.e. 

in _run.py change https://github.com/kkroening/ffmpeg-python/blob/master/ffmpeg/_run.py#L205 to 

`stdin_stream = subprocess.PIPE`

in _probe.py change https://github.com/kkroening/ffmpeg-python/blob/master/ffmpeg/_probe.py#L16 to 

`p = subprocess.Popen(args, stdin=subprocess.PIPE, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE)`

I don't know if this covers all use cases or has any side effects, happy to PR the above though if you want me to.

issue_title: Get calendar of specific user/room
issue_body:
What's a nice way of getting a `Calendar` object corresponding to a specific mailbox email address?

In order to get the calendar of a specific user/room, I need to make a GetFolder SOAP request like follows:

```xml
<m:GetFolder>
<m:FolderShape>
<t:BaseShape>Default</t:BaseShape>
<t:AdditionalProperties>
...
</t:AdditionalProperties>
</m:FolderShape>
<m:FolderIds>
<t:DistinguishedFolderId Id="calendar">
<t:Mailbox>
<t:EmailAddress>rmbx.5335327.CS@uni.au.dk</t:EmailAddress>
</t:Mailbox>
</t:DistinguishedFolderId>
</m:FolderIds>
</m:GetFolder>
```

Currently I make this request as follows:

```python
class DistinguishedFolderOfMailbox(DistinguishedFolderId):
    def to_xml(self, version):
        elem = super().to_xml(version)
        elem.append(self.mailbox.to_xml(version))
        return elem

    folder_id = 1

additional_fields = [
    FieldPath(field=f)
    for f in Calendar.supported_fields(version=account.version)]
                                                                 
a = DistinguishedFolderOfMailbox(id='calendar')
a.mailbox = Mailbox(email_address=self.calendar_email_address)
                                                                 
for elem in GetFolder(account=account).call(
    folders=[a],
    additional_fields=additional_fields,
    shape=IdOnly
):
    ...
```

This way of subclassing DistinguishedFolderId and giving a dummy `folder_id` feels very much like a hack. If exchangelib has better support for this kind of request, I would like to know!

issue_title: Change default marshaller priority so that builtin marshallers have priority over user provided ones
issue_body: Previously, marshallers provided by a user to MarshallerCollection took priority over the builtin marshallers. issue_title: Please follow Semantic Versioning in the future
issue_body:
https://semver.org/

Your recent update to version `2.1.0` breaks backwards compatibility by swapping the order of parameters to the constructor and raising an exception in existing code where no such exception was raised on `2.0.x` versions of the library.

Per the semantic versioning guidelines, the "1" in `2.1.0` should imply that _new features_ were added which are _backwards compatible_ to previous `2.x.x` releases. This breaking change _should_ cause the version number to be upgraded to `3.0.0` as the API is not backwards compatible to a prior `2.x.x` release such as `2.0.18`

issue_title: incorrect join types for nullable translated relations with an OR Q query
issue_body:
Assume:
I have a database that keeps track of movies and tv shows
Movies and tv shows are stored in the same table.
movies have genres, (M2M field) and tv shows have a relation to Series which have genres (M2M field)
Genre's are translatable, but VideoAsset isn't

I want to filter to assets that belong to a given genre.

`genre_query = Q(genres__in=list_genres) | Q(series__genres__in=list_genres)`

`VideoAssets.objects.filter(genre_query)`
will generate a query that uses LEFT JOIN to join the two genre tables.

`get_translation_aware_manager(VideoAssets).filter(genre_query)` on the other hand will use INNER JOIN's and as a result no results would be obtained.

issue_title: ConcurrentRotatingFileHandler chmod will fail across many users
issue_body:
Suppose I have a script which can by run by many users, which uses ConcurrentRotatingFileHandler to the same log file. You've provide "chmod" to allow us to set permissions to ensure it is made writable by both users, but there are 2 problems:

1. Even if the file is made writable, "chmod" is not allowed by another process (on linux at least?). So if root creates the file and the script chmods it to open up the permissions, then if some other user runs the script, their "chmod" with throw an exception.
2. A small race condition on file creation, where there log is created and then afterward the permissions are changed.

issue_body:
Wrong:

```
# current repo doc:  https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/tree/master/R-package
cran <- getOption("repos")
cran["dmlc"] <- "https://apache-mxnet.s3-accelerate.dualstack.amazonaws.com/R/CRAN/"
options(repos = cran)
install.packages("mxnet",dependencies = T)
```

Correct:
```
cran <- getOption("repos")
cran["dmlc"] <- "https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/apache-mxnet/R/CRAN/"
options(repos = cran)
install.packages("mxnet",dependencies = T)
```

## Reference

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/43872455/mxnet-package-installation-in-r

issue_title: use R3.3.0 and im2rec.py to run cnn and always failed
issue_body:
this is my code below,I have run it many times,but it always failed and my computer stopped working, I check my memories,my memories are 8GB
it failed because my memories are not enough? or some other problems?
please answer me how can I run it successfully

library(mxnet)

train = mx.io.ImageRecordIter(
  path.imglist="C:/train1/train1.lst",
  path.imgrec="C:/train1/train1.rec",
  batch.size=4,
  #round.batch=TRUE,
  data.shape=c(224,224,1),
  preprocess.threads=1
)

test = mx.io.ImageRecordIter(
  path.imglist="C:/test1/test1.lst",
  path.imgrec="C:/test1/test1.rec",
  batch.size=4,
  data.shape=c(224,224,1),
  #round.batch=FALSE,
  preprocess.threads=1
)


data <- mx.symbol.Variable('data')

conv1 <- mx.symbol.Convolution(data=data, kernel=c(5,5), num_filter=20)
tanh1 <- mx.symbol.Activation(data=conv1, act_type="tanh")
pool1 <- mx.symbol.Pooling(data=tanh1, pool_type="max",kernel=c(2,2), stride=c(2,2))

conv2 <- mx.symbol.Convolution(data=pool1, kernel=c(5,5), num_filter=50)

tanh2 <- mx.symbol.Activation(data=conv2, act_type="tanh")
pool2 <- mx.symbol.Pooling(data=tanh2, pool_type="max",kernel=c(2,2), stride=c(2,2))

flatten <- mx.symbol.Flatten(data=pool2)
fc1 <- mx.symbol.FullyConnected(data=flatten, num_hidden=500)
tanh3 <- mx.symbol.Activation(data=fc1, act_type="tanh")

fc2 <- mx.symbol.FullyConnected(data=tanh3, num_hidden=10)


lenet <- mx.symbol.SoftmaxOutput(data=fc2)

mx.set.seed(0)

model <- mx.model.FeedForward.create(lenet, X=train, y=train.y,
ctx=mx.cpu(), num.round=5, array.batch.size=1,
learning.rate=0.05, momentum=0.9, wd=0.00001,
eval.metric=mx.metric.accuracy,
epoch.end.callback=mx.callback.log.train.metric(100))


preds <- predict(model, test.array)

pred.label <- max.col(t(preds)) - 1

table(test_org[,1],pred.label)

sum(diag(table(test_org[,1],pred.label)))/1000
--------------------------------------------------------------------end

issue_title: Cannot recognize Intel MPI
issue_body:
The MPI launcher (dmlc-core/tracker/dmlc_tracker/mpi.py) in dmlc-core can not recognize Intel MPI.
It reports ''Unknown MPI Version''

issue_title: Unable to install mxnet with matlab on win10
issue_body:
This should probably be read more as an issue with the documentation, rather than a technical obstacle. While clearly not being sufficiently computer savvy to make my way through this installation, I do consider myself quite technically minded, and I am probably representative of a pretty large potential user group (Matlab on Windows is a pretty common setup, after all). 

The pre-built packages do not contain the matlab component. If it is possible to combine the matlab component from a new version with a pre-built (old) version, then that is not mentioned anywhere (and it seems pretty unlikely anyway).

That leaves building mxnet on windows "by hand". 

Going to the guide (http://newdocs.readthedocs.io/en/latest/build.html#building-on-windows), an immediate problem seems to be that it was written a while ago. I have been unable to find a version of VS community 2013 older than the "Visual C++ Compiler Nov 2013 CTP". When the installed version is newer than the patch, the patch won't install. Presumably, this means that the patch isn't needed? For the record, I have used VS 2013 downloaded from https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkId=517284. issue_title: find a bug in mxnet.gluon.utils.clip_global_norm(arrays, max_norm)
issue_body:
Find a bug in mxnet.gluon.utils.clip_global_norm(arrays, max_norm)
```python
def clip_global_norm(arrays, max_norm):
    """Rescales NDArrays so that the sum of their 2-norm is smaller than `max_norm`.
    """
    assert len(arrays) > 0
    ctx = arrays[0].context
    total_norm = ndarray.add_n(*[ndarray.dot(x, x).as_in_context(ctx)
                                 for x in (arr.reshape((-1,)) for arr in arrays)])
    total_norm = ndarray.sqrt(total_norm).asscalar()
    if not np.isfinite(total_norm):
        warnings.warn(UserWarning('nan or inf is detected. Clipping results will be undefined.'),
                      stacklevel=2)
    scale = max_norm / (total_norm + 1e-8)
    if scale < 1.0:
        for arr in arrays:
            arr *= scale
    return total_norm
```
Perhaps the function should return **arrays** rather than **total_norm**
Thx!

issue_title: User-defined formats should be tied to a Spec (not global)
issue_body:
Currently, user-defined formats are defined globally.  This is not ideal when a python client process interfaces with more than one Swagger service with a different set of user-defined formats applying to each service.  In the worst case, the same format name may be used with differing implementations.

Associate user-defined formats with a `bravado_core.spec.Spec` instead.

issue_title: Increase size of hidden layers
issue_body: @lissyx ran the TED data set until epoch 290 he found that the training WER decreases as follows ![ted_wer training](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/12054740/21415966/1dae2298-c80f-11e6-95d9-6b34793a2dbf.png) However, the validation WER decreases as ![ted_wer validation](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/12054740/21415977/304388f8-c80f-11e6-8b57-ada351273c4b.png) What's clear from this is that we are over overfitting the training data. Traditionally, the means to address this is to increase regularization of the model, in our case increase the dropout rate. What's also clear from this is that the current network geometry is not sufficient to reach our goal of 10% WER on the test data, as even upon overfitting the training data the WER on the training data is 20%. So, if we were to take the traditional route of increasing the dropout rate to address overfitting this would cause the WER of the training and validation sets to be closer. However, due to the decreased capacity of the model, it would cause the WER of training, validation, and test to be far from the target of 10%. So another route to address this is to increase the model's capacity. This should lower the WER of the training data set. Hopefully, this will also lower the validation WER. In support of this hypothesis the original DeepSpeech paper trained on the Switchboard corpus (SWB), which is about the same size as the TED corpus, and used a model with a capacity greater than ours. Our model has hidden layers of 494 neurons. However the original DeepSpeech paper used hidden layers of 2048 neurons for a similar task Thus the goal of this issue is to increase the number of neurons in our hidden layers to 2048 while also adjusting the batch size to compensate for the limited GPU memory.

issue_title: force using __dict__ due to type evolution
issue_body:
Updating from version 0.7.2 to version 0.8.0 changed the way how objects using `__slots__` are serialized.
The JSON data now doesn't include the field names anymore, thus changing the object class would break the code.

Additional info is posted here:
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/28073536/jsonpickle-force-using-dict-due-to-type-evolution

If you try the same code in the StackOverflow Question with version 0.7.2 everything works fine.

Is there a way to tell jsonpickle to not use __getstate__ and to include the field names?

issue_title: python-http-client should define it's own error class which can be imported by consuming libraries

#### Issue Summary

If the response is not success (i.e not HTTP2xx) the library throws a urllib HTTPError.
For clients consuming this library, they now need to know that it was implemented in urllib, import that module into their application and handle the error.

#### Steps to Reproduce

2. attempt to handle error raised without importing urllib and without doing a catch all.

* python-http-client Version: master (latest commit: [commit number])

issue_title: Latest version not performing Euclidean Search ?
issue_body:
Hi,

I've been running a version of Annoy that is a few months old on my Mac up until a couple of days ago when I re-installed. I'm now getting near neighbour searches for Euclidean distance that are different to those I got with the old version. They seem to be the results you'd expect from an angular distance search. I ran a brute-force KNN on the same data, and sure enough - the old Annoy lib gets pretty much the same as a KNN euclidean search. The new version doesn't. Could something have broken with the Euclidean search when the Manhattan search was added ?

I'm calling it with:

myIndex = AnnoyIndex(myData, metric="euclidean")

...but the data it returns when queried looks like cosine/angular distance comparison.

I put print statement in the __init__.py to check the metric variable is being passed, and it's certainly getting that far. Can't tell what's happening inside the compiled library though.

I'll dig around in the C code, but my C is so rusty I doubt I'd be able to spot a bug if there's one there.

Ian

issue_title: "New version released" warning
issue_body:
We should add a warning, in the command line and in the webui, that notifies users that a new OQ Engine release is available (for stable releases). This:
 - Can be disabled via confing (`openquake.cfg`)
 - Should silently fail if no connection is available
 - Should not create delays: a cache should be used (a field in the DB is enough...)

In the past this issue was less problematic since everyone was using Linux packages that get updates with `apt` or `yum`. Nowadays most people are using installers which need manual upgrade.

issue_title: EC2 waiter for spot instance when status-code updates
issue_body:
boto3 currently offers waiter for ec2 spot request when requests are fulfilled ([spot_instance_request_fulfilled](https://boto3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/reference/services/ec2.html#EC2.Waiter.SpotInstanceRequestFulfilled)). This waiter returns true only if status-code changes to fulfilled.
In reality, a spot request could also fail or update its status code to holding (price-too-low) or over capacity(capacity-not-available). In that scenario, I would like to re-evaluate my spot request.

It would be great if waiter could take an argument, such that waiter would return when status-code is updated to one of the provided status-code.

issue_title: Submodule paths handled incorrectly when adding
issue_body:
The Submodule class treats all paths as relative to the current working directory, regardless of the actual path to the repository.

This complicates batch operations on repositories involving submodules, as the cwd must be changed and re-changed for each repo.

issue_title: Regex mathing in Redshift
issue_body:
One of the functions that Redshift has and Postgres hasn't is `REGEXP_MATCHES` (see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/redshift/latest/dg/c_unsupported-postgresql-functions.html).

But when calling `fn.RegexpLike`, we get `SELECT REGEXP_MATCHES`, which doesn't work.

Can you please replace it with something like `REGEXP_SUBSTR`?

The Java mocking library supports a wide array of argument matchers. This reduces coupling between the test and parameters irrelevant to the test. For example,

```
    allow(tchannel).thrift(

        timeout=config.get('populous.timeout')
    ).and_return_future(
        mock.Mock(body=T)
    )
```

Similarly, mock also has wildcard support in the form of mock.ANY etc.

issue_title: Hive binary type support
issue_body:
IIUC, PyHive don't provide [Type Constructors](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0249/#type-objects-and-constructors) and it tries decoding bytes using UTF-8. Users cannot insert arbitrary binary buffer. For example,

```
k = b'\xe313'
cursor_write.execute('''
        INSERT INTO TABLE my_db.my_table PARTITION (partition_id = %d)
        VALUES (%s)''', (1, k))
```

These codes will raise

```
UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf8' codec can't decode byte 0xe3 in position 0: invalid continuation byte
```

I am looking for workaround without luck. Is it possible to have a workaround? If not, is it easy for PyHive to support Type Constructors? And is PyHive going to support this?

issue_title: Deploy issue when network uses IPv6
issue_body:
File: pyfrc/lib/pyfrc/robotpy/installer.py

Code (line 366 through 379):

```python
if not no_resolve:
    try:
        print("Looking up hostname", hostname, '...')
        addrs = socket.getaddrinfo(hostname, None)
    except socket.gaierror as e:
        raise Error("Could not find robot at %s" % hostname) from e
        
    # pick the first address that is sock_stream
    for _, t, _, _, (ip, _) in addrs: # <- Error on this line due to (ip, _) not working for IPv6
        if t == socket.SOCK_STREAM:
            print("-> Found %s at %s" % (hostname, ip))
            print()
            hostname = ip
            break 
```

The returned tuple from socket.getaddrinfo() is different depending on if it is IPv4 or IPv6 addresses. 

socket.getaddrinfo() will return two differently formatted tuples, depending on if the address is IPv4 or IPv6:

`IPv4: [(<AddressFamily.AF_INET: 2>, 0, 0, '', ('8.8.8.8', 0))]`
`IPv6: [(<AddressFamily.AF_INET6: 23>, 0, 0, '', ('2001:4860:4860::8888', 0, 0, 0))]`

I do not currently have a system to test with, so this might not be the best way. I believe everything in the control system supports IPv6 since our RoboRIO was resolving with a IPv6 address. I'm not sure if the ssh supports IPv6 or what other implications there are, but the RoboRIO still had an IPv4 address associated with it:

socket.getaddrinfo('roborio-4901-frc.local', None) returns
`[ (<AddressFamily.AF_INET6: 23>, 0, 0, '', ('fe80::280:2fff:fe17:3af1%8', 0, 0, 8)), (<AddressFamily.AF_INET: 2> 0, 0, '', ('129.252.23.54', 0)) ]`

Per the Python socket.getaddrinfo() documentation:

> The function returns a list of 5-tuples with the following structure:
> (family, socktype, proto, canonname, sockaddr) 

How about, instead of unpacking it like this:
```python
for _, t, _, _, (ip, _) in addrs:
    if t == socket.SOCK_STREAM:
        print("-> Found %s at %s" % (hostname, ip))
        print()
        hostname = ip
        break 
```

You unpack it like this:
```python
for _, t, _, _, sockaddr in addrs:
    if t == socket.SOCK_STREAM:
        print("-> Found %s at %s" % (hostname, sockaddr[0]))
        print()
        hostname = sockaddr[0]
        break 
```

If IPv4 doesn't work, you can check the first tuple element to see if it is an AF_INET or AF_INET6:
```python
for family, t, _, _, sockaddr in addrs:
    if family == socket.AddressFamily.AF_INET and t == socket.SOCK_STREAM:
        print("-> Found %s at %s" % (hostname, sockaddr[0]))
        print()
        hostname = sockaddr[0]
        break 
```

issue_title: Howto run with a framework version of python
issue_body:
I'm starting a wxPython project which requires a framework build of Python. I installed 3.6.4 from Python.org including all the options, and have pipenv use it with the `pipenv --python 3.6.4` arguments (followed by a `pipenv install wxpython`).

Pipenv tells us it's using a framework build of python (path contains Python.framework):

> Using /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/bin/python3.6m to create virtualenv…

However the program won't run, it says it's not using a framework build of python.

##### Describe your environment

1. macOs High Sierra 10.13.3
1. Python version: 3.6.4
1. Pipenv version: pipenv, version 9.0.3

##### Expected result

Expect the virtual environment to use the installed framework version of python.

##### Actual result

>This program needs access to the screen. Please run with a
>Framework build of python, and only when you are logged in
>on the main display of your Mac.

##### Steps to replicate

- install python.org 3.6.4 with the default installation options, reinstall pip3; and pipenv, pew and virtualenv with --user.

``` 
mkdir wxpythontest && cd wxpythontest
pipenv --python 3.6.4
pipenv install wxpython
curl https://gist.githubusercontent.com/svandragt/d1f0d6e2b3d19053b940fffecb3de88e/raw/4b4f9cb881f5dab6f97b96d3ca0ef4347a0a218e/hello.py -O hello.py
pipenv run python hello.py
```

issue_title: Improve OpenGraph support
issue_body:
As discussed with @maethu, the OpenGraph viewlet is registered on the OpenGraph marker behavior, which has been enabled on the content page, but not on other simplelayoutish types like `ftw.news`, `ftw.events` or custom types in the policies: https://github.com/4teamwork/ftw.simplelayout/blob/d4d844982e1c967df43042fd540f6a9c073613d6/ftw/simplelayout/contenttypes/profiles/default/types/ftw.simplelayout.ContentPage.xml#L35

Now we could add the behavior in all our other simplelayoutish types. Or better, register the OpenGraph viewlet for `ISimplelayout` so all simplelayoutish types will gain OpenGraph support automatically.

issue_title: Throwing: Uncaught Error: Bad response [content-type: text/html;charset=utf-8] is considered bad idea in one usecase.
issue_body:
Simplelayout JS files are now only loaded when logged in.
But logged in does not means, that the user has sufficient privileges.

issue_title: RFC deposit: filter depositions based on user roles
issue_body:
Problem
=======

Currently for all visitors of the index page of deposit ```/deposit``` all depositions are shown.

We would like to have some filtering done based on the roles of the current user so that different users can see different submissions.


Proposal
===========
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/pu/invenio/modules/deposit/views/deposit.py#L113
```python
ctx = dict(
        deposition_types=DepositionType.all(),
        my_depositions=Deposition.get_depositions(current_user),
        prefill_data=draft_cache.data,
    )
```

Instead of ```DepositionType.all()``` we can have a new function ```DepositionType.all_authorized(current_user)``` that only returns deposition types the user is allowed to see.

We will need a new attribute in the ``DepositionType`` model that represents the roles that are authorized to see such Deposition type (we probably don't need a config variable for this, if a new role needs to be added to a deposition type, it can be changed in the DepositionType definition)

Then for all views that access a certain deposition type, like:

```python
@blueprint.route('/<depositions:deposition_type>')
@blueprint.route('/<depositions:deposition_type>/create',
                 methods=['POST', 'GET'])
@blueprint.route('/<depositions:deposition_type>/<int:uuid>/<draft_id>',
                 methods=['POST'])
```

There should be a protection so that, something like a 404 is returned if the user is not authorized. Should this be implemented as a decorator for those views?

What more should be taken into account? Suggestions for the implementation?

issue_title: support pip installation
issue_body:
there is a problem if one tries to build the Python extension using the `pip` installer. The problem is that the setup.py is not located in the root of the repository, but in the `python` binding subfolder. The way pip works is to copy the source files to a temporary folder and try to build from there. But since the C/C++ source files are located higher in the repository tree (`../enc`, `../dec`) relative to setup.py, then pip does not (cannot?) copy these over to its temporary build folder, and therefore it fails to build the extension.

A solution would be to make a hard copy (instead of a symlink) of the `enc` and `dec` folders inside the `python` subfolder. I believe git can efficiently handle such duplicate files and store them under the same object, as long as they have the same content.

That means one would have to synchronise it every time there is a change. I don't know if anyone has a better solution...

The reason I want to use pip is to allow publishing the Brotli extension to the official Python Package Index (PyPI) repository. Once that is done, one could simply do `pip install brotli` to download, build and install the extension. Besides, one could add brotli to the list of dependencies for other packages (e.g. fontTools, etc.).

I wonder whether @khaledhosny has already thought about publishing the Brotli extension to PyPI?

I'm already experimenting in this direction in https://github.com/anthrotype/brotli-wheels
I'm trying to use Travis and Appveyor to automatically build pre-compiled Python [wheel](http://pythonwheels.com/) packages for Windows and OS X.

Please let me know what you think.
Thank you,

Cosimo

### Icon request checklist

- [single ] This is a single icon or matched pair (Ex: `lock` / `unlock`)
- [ yes] The title starts with "Icon request: " and is followed by the requested icon name (Ex: `Icon request: magical-unicorn`)
- [ yes] I have [searched for existing issues](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/issues) and to the best of my knowledge this is not a duplicate

issue_title: Acceptance tests do not honor PATRONI_POSTGRESQL_BIN_DIR
issue_body:
The `stop` method in `features/environment.py` runs `pg_ctl` via `subprocess.call`; this apparently does not honor the `$PATRONI_POSTGRESQL_BIN_DIR` environment variable even if set, resulting in a failure in the `check immediate failover when master_start_timeout=0` scenario:

```
  Scenario: check immediate failover when master_start_timeout=0  # features/basic_replication.feature:31
    Given I kill postmaster on postgres2                          # features/steps/basic_replication.py:22
      Traceback (most recent call last):
        File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/behave/model.py", line 1456, in run
          match.run(runner.context)
        File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/behave/model.py", line 1903, in run
          self.func(context, *args, **kwargs)
        File "features/steps/basic_replication.py", line 24, in stop_postgres
          return context.pctl.stop(name, postgres=True)
        File "features/environment.py", line 527, in wrapper
          return getattr(self._processes[name], func)(*args, **kwargs)
        File "features/environment.py", line 133, in stop
          return subprocess.call(['pg_ctl', '-D', self._data_dir, 'stop', '-mi', '-w'])
        File "/usr/lib/python3.5/subprocess.py", line 247, in call
          with Popen(*popenargs, **kwargs) as p:
        File "/usr/lib/python3.5/subprocess.py", line 676, in __init__
          restore_signals, start_new_session)
        File "/usr/lib/python3.5/subprocess.py", line 1289, in _execute_child
          raise child_exception_type(errno_num, err_msg)
      FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'pg_ctl'
```

If I hack in a check for that and then set it, the acceptance tests pass for me even if `$PATH` does not include the postgres binaries (I have not fully figured out why that is sometimes the case, it seems to be when I have to `su` to user `postgres` in order to avoid running the testsuite as `root`).

Excuse my python, but here's a PoC patch for this:

```
--- ./features/environment.py.orig	2017-09-29 14:54:22.558666638 +0200
+++ ./features/environment.py	2017-09-29 14:58:32.971607543 +0200
@@ -130,7 +130,11 @@
 
     def stop(self, kill=False, timeout=15, postgres=False):
         if postgres:
-            return subprocess.call(['pg_ctl', '-D', self._data_dir, 'stop', '-mi', '-w'])
+            cmd = 'pg_ctl'
+            pgbindir = os.environ['PATRONI_POSTGRESQL_BIN_DIR']
+            if pgbindir is not None:
+                cmd = pgbindir + '/pg_ctl'
+            return subprocess.call([cmd, '-D', self._data_dir, 'stop', '-mi', '-w'])
         super(PatroniController, self).stop(kill, timeout)
         if self.watchdog:
             self.watchdog.stop()
```

issue_title: Clean up methods which return self
issue_body:
See https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/pull/770/files#r27422508

`return self` is a JavaScript-y method chaining construct.

In Python

```python
foo = Foo()
foo2 = foo.update()
```

indicates that `foo2` is indeed a `Foo` object, but that `foo.update()` has applied the changes without mutating `foo`. Using `return self` breaks that expectation.

For example on `list`

```python
>>> a = [3, -4, 1, 0]
>>> b = a.sort()
>>> b is None
True
>>> a
[-4, 0, 1, 3]
```

I am running mapreduce on python app engine that throws alot of exceptions like:

The job uses a GoogleCloudStorageRecordInputReader for reading data and GoogleCloudStorageConsistentRecordOutputWriter for writing.

The input file sizes range from 1 MB to 200 MB. The mapper/combiner/reducer functions are very simple string processing with no with no calls to external services like datastore. Each input record is a string of less than a couple hundred bytes.

The job finishes correctly in the end though I suspect it runs longer than necessary due to all of the instance shutdowns from exceeding memory available. In a job running right now, I've had 12 soft memory limit exceptions that shut down an instance and the job is not yet done.

I'm not sure what is driving all of the memory use. Could it result from buffering on the readers or writers?
Other likely causes?

issue_title: Adding batch image processing to Vision API
issue_body:
The amount of data that Vision can return can be pretty deep and processing more than one image at a time adds another level to that.

Here's my proposed approach to work with multiple images with multiple feature types.

```python
        >>> images = client.images()
        >>> for i in range(10):
        ...     images.add_image(client.image(source_uri='gs://%s.jpg' % (i,)))
        >>> images.detect_faces()
        {'0.jpg': [],
          '1.jpg': [],}

        >>> features = [Feature(FeatureTypes.FACE_DETECTION, 5),
        ...             Feature(FeatureTypes.LOGO_DETECTION, 3)]
        >>> for i in range(10):
        ...     image = client.image(source_uri='gs://%s.jpg' % (i,))
        ...     images.add_image(image=image, features=features)
        >>> images.detect()
```

With a context manager it could look something like this...

```python
        >>> features = [Feature(FeatureTypes.FACE_DETECTION, 5),
        ...             Feature(FeatureTypes.LOGO_DETECTION, 3)]
        >>> with client.images() as images:
        ...     for i in range(10):
        ...         image = client.image(source_uri='gs://%s.jpg' % (i,))
        ...         images.add_image(image=image, features=features)
        ...     results = images.detect()
```

issue_title: pex.resolvable.InvalidRequirement: Unknown requirement type: .
issue_body: It used to be possible to clone a python repository and do 'pex .' (in other words, check if path is an exploded source package) and build it.  Not sure when this behavior changed -- breaks some packaging scripts I've been using for periscope.

issue_title: Use a [GLOBAL] pants.ini section for global option values
issue_body:
Currently we use [DEFAULT], which can get confusing, because DEFAULT values are inherited by all other sections, and this overlaps confusingly with the more general option value inheritance mechanism in the options system itself.

Sites can still use [DEFAULT] if they want that config inheritance, but at least it will no longer trigger unintentionally.

issue_title: `excludes` are not rolled into the hash of `jar_dependency`
Exercise PEX chroot copy vs link and pre-compile vs. no in the python backend
issue_body: #1440 brought in options to both copy instead of hardlink in pex chroots as well as pre-compile or not.  Plumb options for these or otherwise use the options directly in python backend code to make chroots more efficient / well-tuned to pants usage of them.

issue_title: Executing commands on a slave zone
issue_body:
Many commands only apply to a master (coordinator) zone and you would not expect a user to execute them on a slave. However people do! 
Below is a list of commands tested against a slave zone, and at the bottom one against a zone that has volume set to fixed (typically a ZP90 driving an external amp). It shows the command and the behaviour / error that occurs.

Maybe this behaviour is what we should expect, and users should trap it?
Or should we do nothing and just return (as play and stop do)?
Or should we raise a trappable exception with a clear message that it should not be executed on a slave?
Or......

Welcome thoughts
Cheers David

```
1) commands executed on a slave:

    play               does nothing - ok?
    stop               does nothing - ok?

    play_uri           un_joins zone to be a master and plays -ok?
    play_from_queue    un_joins zone to be a master and plays -ok?
    switch_to_line_in  un_joins zone to be a master and sets to line in -ok?

    pause              soco.exceptions.SoCoUPnPException: UPnP Error 701 received: Transition not available from 192.168.1.69
    seek               soco.exceptions.SoCoUPnPException: UPnP Error 701 received: Transition not available from 192.168.1.69

issue_title: Make the serialiser match the HTML fragment serialization algorithm
issue_body:
[Linkity link](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/syntax.html#html-fragment-serialisation-algorithm).

issue_title: API / controller logging should be via logspout
issue_body:
I haven't dug into the code too deeply yet, but conversation in #3947 exposed that `deis-controller` logs are written _directly_ to a volume that it shares with `deis-logger`.  This means that the route `deis-controller` logs take to their resting place on that volume is _different_ than the route taken by application logs making their way to the same place.  In the case of an application, it's via `STDOUT` --> `deis-logspout` --> `deis-logger` --> the volume.

There's a slight problem that arises from this discrepancy.  In the increasingly common scenario where an operator runs "Ceph-less," Deis is installed sans the storage subsystem components and adequate, external alternatives are provided for those components _and_ any other Deis component that expresses a direct dependency on the storage subsystem (e.g. `deis-database`).  When opting to run this way, `deis-logger` is among the components not installed.  A consequence of this is that `deis-logspout` is configured to forward logs _directly_ to an external log service.  Since application logs are forwarded via `deis-logspout`, those logs all make it to the external service.  `deis-controller` logs, on the other hand, since they take a different route, would never make it to the external log service.

It seems this could be easily corrected by transitioning `deis-controller` to writing its logs via `STDOUT` and `logspout` just as any application does.  This would increase consistency and guarantee that whether running with or without Ceph and `deis-logger`, all logs ultimately land in one common place via a consistent path.

In #3947 @bacongobbler has noted some possible problems with this approach, but we should probably move that discussion here.

issue_title: Do not update/query if property already set
issue_body:
when user creates a new lun with `lun_id` or `name`, storops should not fetch all properties from remote, instead, only return the value set by user:

```python
In [4]: import storops

In [5]: vnx = storops.VNXSystem('192.168.1.50', 'sysadmin', 'sysadmin')

In [6]: lun = vnx.get_lun(lun_id=1)

In [7]: lun.lun_id
No handlers could be found for logger "storops.vnx.navi_command"
Out[7]: 1

In [8]:

```

You can see, a query was set when `lun.lun_id`

Thanks
Peter

issue_title: Storops cannot be certified for OSP10
issue_body:
In current implementation, storops depends on PyYAML >= 3.11.

However, the latest version of PyYAML in OSP10 is 3.10.

issue_title: Update $PATH in .zshrc to find conda
issue_body: I'm using [zsh](github.com/robbyrussell/oh-my-zsh), and just ran the Miniconda install script according to [this guide](http://conda.pydata.org/docs/install/quick.html) to install conda. While it updates the `$PATH` variable in my `.bash_profile` to look for the conda binary, I have to copy and paste over the `export PATH=...` declaration in my `.zshrc`. This is obviously not a huge deal, but it would be great if the install script could do this automatically!

issue_title: Consider removing tab completion to improve startup time
issue_body:
Several users have recently noticed slow conda startup times.  It seems the major reason we load so much code at startup is to support argparse's tab completion.

See https://groups.google.com/a/continuum.io/forum/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer#!msg/conda/UU8MHIoGMqA/FqSy4SpSAQAJ for one recent discussion.

If the tradeoff is tab completion or startup time, my inclination is that most users would go for startup time.

Thoughts?

CC @msarahan @ilanschnell @mingwandroid

issue_title: File locking on Windows

issue_title: Ambiguous package resolution for X.Y and X.Y.0
issue_body:
Hello everyone,

I'm not sure if this is a duplicate, but I ran across something pretty unintuitive. It involves packages that have a version of `X.Y` and `X.Y.0` available. Specifically, there is a package on the `bioconda` channel that has both a `1.4.0` version and a `1.4` version. I'd like to install `1.4.0`, however conda reports that there are 2 package resolution options and will randomly switch which choice it makes. 

I'm not sure why `1.4` would be chosen as I requested the more specific `1.4.0` (I could see how the opposite would be ambiguous however). I suppose this has to do with PEP440's implicit `.0` versions, but I'm still not sure how to disambiguate these cases. Are these versions incorrectly unique in filename, but semantically (according to PEP440) identical?

```

(q2-dev) 13:23:09 § conda install -c bioconda "bioconductor-dada2=1.4.0"
Fetching package metadata ...........
Solving package specifications: .
Warning: 2 possible package resolutions (only showing differing packages):
  - bioconda::bioconductor-dada2-1.4.0-r3.3.1_0
  - bioconda::bioconductor-dada2-1.4-r3.3.1_0

Package plan for installation in environment /home/evan/.conda/envs/q2-dev:

The following packages will be UPDATED:

    bioconductor-dada2: 1.2-r3.3.1_0 bioconda --> 1.4.0-r3.3.1_0 bioconda

Proceed ([y]/n)? n

Exiting
```

Fetching package metadata ...........
Solving package specifications: .
Warning: 2 possible package resolutions (only showing differing packages):
  - bioconda::bioconductor-dada2-1.4-r3.3.1_0
  - bioconda::bioconductor-dada2-1.4.0-r3.3.1_0

Package plan for installation in environment /home/evan/.conda/envs/q2-dev:
