issue_title: mtDNA variant presentation in Scout
issue_body:
Sorry for not being clear in my first issue (#731). I wrote transcripts but meant genes in this case. MtDNA only has one "transcript" despite all genes.

All cases, analyzed after you fixed the problem with the missing mtDNA variants, are now presented really strange in Scout. 

Since mtDNA only has one transcript all variants have earlier only been showed connected to one gene. Not all other upstream or downstream genes, since this means all other genes in mtDNA are there. If  they are all there it is impossible to see the important information.

I have attached a pdf with screenshots and more information. Could you please check this out. We need to solve this as soon as possible. Thanks!

[Issue mtDNA in Scout.pdf](https://github.com/Clinical-Genomics/scout/files/1723810/Issue.mtDNA.in.Scout.pdf)

/Helene

issue_title: Place the short argument in front of long argument in help document.
issue_body:
Here's what our argument help looks like:
```
Arguments
    --destination-blob -b      [Required]: Name of the destination blob. If the exists, it will be
                                           overwritten.
    --destination-container -c [Required]: The container name.
```

Note that the order of the arguments is long first and then short one follows. It is inconsistent with common linux manuals, in which short arguments are in the front and long arguments are aligned.
```
11:44 $ curl --help
Usage: curl [options...] <url>
Options: (H) means HTTP/HTTPS only, (F) means FTP only
     --anyauth       Pick "any" authentication method (H)
 -a, --append        Append to target file when uploading (F/SFTP)
     --basic         Use HTTP Basic Authentication (H)
     --cacert FILE   CA certificate to verify peer against (SSL)
     --capath DIR    CA directory to verify peer against (SSL)
 -E, --cert CERT[:PASSWD]  Client certificate file and password (SSL)
     --cert-status   Verify the status of the server certificate (SSL)
     --cert-type TYPE  Certificate file type (DER/PEM/ENG) (SSL)
     --ciphers LIST  SSL ciphers to use (SSL)
     --compressed    Request compressed response (using deflate or gzip)
 -K, --config FILE   Read config from FILE
```

The latter format is more readable since there short arguments easily stand out.

issue_title: DeprecationWarning: invalid escape sequence \w (aiohttp/client_reqrep.py:41)
issue_body:
## Long story short

Running python3.6 with all warnings enabled, and before aiohttp 3.3.2 has been compiled to .pyc files:

```
py.warnings WARNING  /usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/aiohttp/client_reqrep.py:41: DeprecationWarning: invalid escape sequence \w
  json_re = re.compile('^application/(?:[\w.+-]+?\+)?json')
```

The fix is to make the regex a raw string, i.e. 

```
json_re = re.compile(r'^application/(?:[\w.+-]+?\+)?json')
```

issue_title: Use loop.create_future() instead direct instantiation
issue_body: Recent asyncio added new function `loop.create_future()` for `Future` object creation, so third party loops like `uvloop` can create own faster future implementations. We need to use this approach to benefit from speed ups.

 min 1 of { /Common/https_443 /Common/icmp }

````

 min 1 of  /Common/https_443 /Common/icmp

issue_title: Basket Line Reference with already encoded utf-8 options
issue_body:
In an ecommerce model with Options a Basket Line Reference is created by its options representation encoded as utf-8. It raises a problem when option value is already encoded in utf-8. 

```python
    def _create_line_reference(self, product, stockrecord, options):
        """
        Returns a reference string for a line based on the item
        and its options.
        """
        base = '%s_%s' % (product.id, stockrecord.id)
        if not options:
            return base
        return "%s_%s" % (base, zlib.crc32(repr(options).encode('utf8')))
```

I imagine this encoding is done because only basket.AttributeLine value supported is charField. In extended models with more rich Attribute values it is a problem.

issue_title: Signature verification ignores the Key ID in the signature
issue_body:
### Summary
`keys.verify_signature` takes a **key** and a **signature** (along with the data over which the signature may have been signed). Signature verification determines only whether or not the signature value (`sig`) can have been produced by the provided **key**. It does **not** check whether or not the signature itself reports that it was made using that key, so the keyid listed in the signature provided need not match the key in order for the signature to be deemed valid. Correct behavior, I would say, is to deem the signature invalid if the key id it lists is not the key id that is expected, without bothering to determine if the signature value ('sig') is correct.

### Example
Suppose I have two keys, k1 and k2. I use k1 to sign data, and then change the signature dictionary to claim that I used k2 by listing k2's key id, even though I signed with k1. I send you the signature. You want to know if it was generated by k1. You run `keys.verify_signature` and everything checks out, even though the signature incorrectly claims to be a signature by k2.

### Security Implications
Note that this can mean that an attacker could have a signature deemed valid without knowing what the right keyid is, and that, in upstream code, a single signature can be deemed valid if it could have been generated by any acceptable key, without the attacker having to choose one key or attempt multiple times listing different key ids. That problem depends on the behavior of upstream code, though, which can be written to eliminate that possibility.

While this may not be a **large** security issue -- the signature value itself must still match a signature produced by the provided key over the provided data -- it's confusing, may break assumptions in upstream code, and may have **some** security impact upstream (where you can use the same signature in the same signature verification request to try to match any acceptable key's signature without choosing a single key).

https://github.com/secure-systems-lab/securesystemslib/blob/433670b84f9d1490bacfcd2ba67b9ec77df0d970/securesystemslib/keys.py#L940-L946

issue_title: Multipart Media Responses Don't Work in Python3
issue_body:
The multipart parser doesn't work with Python3
https://github.com/refindlyllc/rets/blob/master/rets/parsers/get_object.py#L9

In short, bytes=string in Py2 and unicode=string in Py3. So we need to decode the response.content in Py3, do the splitting up of the images, and then encode the individual images back to bytes for saving as valid images. The encode/decode process is a little tricky.

issue_title: Add no-zoneinfo build option
issue_body:
Currently the `zoneinfo` package is only really necessary if you want to use *our* zoneinfo files, which I suspect are mostly used either out of ignorance or on Windows (which does not ship zoneinfo).

Ideally, we'd have an option to install a version of `dateutil` *without* `zoneinfo` (no files, and without the module). I'm not really sure how to do this, however. There's the [`extras-require`](https://setuptools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/setuptools.html#declaring-extras-optional-features-with-their-own-dependencies) option that lets you have some sort of conditional build dependencies, but I don't know if there's a way to have "extras" that essentially **take away** features, rather than add them.

issue_title: Apispec doesn't play nicely with the schemas where fields are generated/modified on the fly
Add option to throw validation error on extra keys
issue_body:
I'm not sure if I'm missing something, but I would like to throw error if extra keys are provided, as in:

```python
>>> class AlbumSchema(Schema):
...     title = fields.Str()

>>> AlbumSchema(strict=True).load({'extra': 2}, allow_extra=False)
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
marshmallow.exceptions.ValidationError: {'_schema': ["Extra arguments passed: ['extra']"]}
```

I've been using the following implementation (taken from https://github.com/sloria/webargs/issues/87#issuecomment-183949558):

```python
class BaseSchema(Schema):

    @pre_load
    def validate_extra(self, in_data):
        if not isinstance(in_data, dict):
            return

        extra_args = [key for key in in_data.keys() if key not in self.fields]
        if extra_args:
            raise ValidationError('Extra arguments passed: {}'.format(extra_args))
```

I would expect this to be a common need, however, so could it be supported by the library out-of-the-box?

issue_title: Improve sorting for edge case of match-all
issue_body:
The issue is described in [comment](https://github.com/aichaos/rivescript-python/pull/69#issuecomment-302452662). The desired sorting should be following. The soft-rule is `only by the number of words that aren't wildcards, because that's usually the more useful thing when trying to order triggers by "most specific first" ordering`
```
+ [*] text [*]
- match with text
+ [*]
- match all 1
+ *
- match all 2
```

issue_title: Add input option to load ERC190 compliant package file for analysis
issue_body:
## Description: 
I am making an educated guess here, but the `-t/--truffle` option looks like it enables loading compiled bytecode and/or deployed contract addresses (collectively the "smart contract system") for analysis using the mythril engine. I would suggest making a more general option to load ERC190 compliant package files (ethPM spec) for use in this analysis.

This could eventually be merged into a plugin-based system used by Populus such that it becomes part of a Pythonic developer process

## Link
https://github.com/ethpm/py-ethpm for the python implementation of ethPM spec

## Implementation Notes:
I would suggest `-p/--package`

issue_title: Custom Authorization implementation ignored?
issue_body:
My current project is in need of a custom authorization implementation so I started to write one from the examples in the (excellent) documentation, but as far as I can tell I cannot make it apply on my resources  simply by following the documentation.

I have narrowed it down to this rather draconian example implementation:

```
class MyOwnAuthorization(Authorization):
    def read_list(self, object_list, bundle):
        raise Unauthorized("denied")
        return []

    def read_detail(self, object_list, bundle):
        raise Unauthorized("denied.")
        return False

    # same goes for update_list, update_detail, create_list, create_detail, delete_list and delete_detail
```

In my resources, I have applied them using the Meta class:

```
class ProgressResource(Resource):
    # ...
    class Meta:
        resource_name = 'progresses'
        allowed_methods = ["get", "post", "delete", "put"]
        authentication = ApiKeyAuthentication()
        serializer = Serializer(formats=['json', 'xml'])
        authorization = MyOwnAuthorization()
```

Some more inputs:

 * Django 1.7.1, 
 * django-tastypie 1.12.1

What am I missing here? `MyOwnAuthorization` should always return HTTP 401, shouldn't it?

issue_title: wrap Optimizer as Estimator for grid search and cross validation

issue_title: get-value fails with no model
issue_body:
[Thanks to Joshua Gancher; his feedback on SBV led to this example.]

Here's an interesting corner case, where we try to get a value that is defined after `check-sat` is issued:

```
(set-logic ALL)
(set-option :produce-models true)
(check-sat)
(define-fun a () Int 1)
(get-value (a))
```

For this Z3 says:

```
sat
(error "line 4 column 14: model is not available")
```

According to the spec, I think we should correctly get the value of `1` here. Indeed, CVC4 and mathsat both produce the value `1` correctly.

This isn't just curiosity, unfortunately. This comes up when Z3 is being used from SBV where the code is generated and we might query for such expressions after check-sat is issued.

Thanks!

issue_title: Z3Py Problem With Bit-Blasing
issue_body:
Hello, I'm using Z3Py to convert SMT expressions into SAT. However, when I run the following code:

from z3 import *
g = Goal()

x = BitVec('x', 3)
g.add(And(x >= 0, x < 2)) 

t = Then('simplify', 'bit-blast')
subgoal = t(g)
assert len(subgoal) == 1
print subgoal[0].sexpr()

I get the following output:
(goal
  (not k!2)
  (not (not (or k!2 (not k!1)))))

If you look closely you'll notice that the only satisfying assignment is when both k!1 and k!2 are 0. However, this should not be the case as there should be two satisfying assignments, x == 0 and x ==1.

issue_title: Recurrent layers: Accept layer for hid_init
issue_body:
The recurrent layers currently have some custom behaviour when `hid_init` is a TensorVariable rather than a shared variable, callable or numpy array: They assume `hid_init` is a tensor of one order higher than otherwise, to include the batch dimension, and they assume `hid_init` is not to be learned.

With #404, parameters can be arbitrary Theano expressions anyway, and overriding that behaviour to assume a different dimensionality is a bit awkward. As discussed in https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/issues/11#issuecomment-139221388 and following, a better solution would be allowing `hid_init` to be a `Layer`, and assuming it would include the batch dimension in this case. This would also be a step towards supporting the encoder-decoder architecture discussed in https://github.com/Lasagne/Lasagne/issues/391#issuecomment-144549176 and following.

issue_title: Update read-Allen-Brain-Oservatory notebook
issue_body:
As notebook are usually considered a great source of examples, researchers will often copy snippet as a starting point.

Here are few suggestions to improve them:

* [ ] add a cell briefly explaining the different between NWB 1.0 and NWB 2.0. And reference documentation
* [ ] select a style. See #251
* [ ] re-organize import. See below
* [ ] Use print as a function. ``print(foo)`` instead of ``print foo``


### Import order

See https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/#imports

Instead of:

```
from allensdk.core.brain_observatory_cache import BrainObservatoryCache
import allensdk.brain_observatory.stimulus_info as si
import warnings
import pynwb
import pandas as pd
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
```

prefer

```
import warnings

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt

import pynwb
from allensdk.core.brain_observatory_cache import BrainObservatoryCache
import allensdk.brain_observatory.stimulus_info as si

issue_title: LHCb uses dirac-proxy-init rather than lhcb-proxy-init
issue_body:
From what I can see, LHCb users call `dirac-proxy-init` as the LHCb plugin requires the `DiracProxy` credential to run stuff which has `dirac-proxy-init` hardcoded. Just talked to the Grid guys and this is a bad idea as in some cases, this puts RFC proxies in that are no longer supported. They suggest that the best way is to **always** call `lhcb-proxy-init` and the best way to do this that I can see is to create another Credential type (`LHCbProxy`) that calls this. 

What do people think?

P.S. This isn't massively urgent but needs to be fixed in the next couple of weeks!

issue_title: Number support
issue_body:
For an intent parser I created, where you could ask the timetable for a specific bus line, I had to write down the possible numbers for bus lines. But then, typing real numbers from the command line, e.g. `when is the next bus sixty-one leaving from home?` starts to be annoying.

Would it be possible to build in a support for numbers, somehow? I know of the library [inflect](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/inflect) that translates integers to text, which would help quite a bit on this. There is also [pynum2word](https://sourceforge.net/projects/pynum2word/).

I think that being able to specify a new sort of entity (i.e. a numerical entity) would be nice. Something where you specify a range, say 1 to 100, and it will then understand both `1` and `sixty-one`, preferably returning the integer version in the json structure.

issue_title: Reset login attempts on successful login
issue_body:
Hi @aleksihakli

We ran in to the problem that failed login attempts do not get deleted on successful user login.

Let's say a user misstypes his password from time to time but between the failed logins he logs in correctly. Like this the failed logins count up and it could happen that in the future when he fails to login he gets locked out on his first try.

We fixed this with a custom login view:

```python
class CustomLoginView(LoginView):
    def form_valid(self, form):
        valid = super().form_valid(form)
        user = form.get_user()

        if valid:
            reset(username=user.email)

        return valid
```

Would it make sense to have such a functionality as a option in `settings.py`?

issue_title: Default behaviour for static bidirectional_rnns with return_seq=True is strange.
issue_body:
Basically its weird to me that the bi-directional RNN (a) produces a native python list of states rather than a 3-D Tensor and (b) seems to permute the samples and timesteps dimensions.

I figured out how to get things back to "normal" from line 107 of recurrent.py where the author uses tf.pack() and tf.transpose() before slicing off the unused indices from the dynamic results. However the normal behaviour for static rnns is just to return in the state I mentioned above.

I'm still a relative newcomer to the TensorFlow game since I started using it about a year ago. So, happy to be told I'm wrong on this one. However, for newbs like me maybe it would be good to have clearer docs since the [documentation on recurrent layers](http://tflearn.org/layers/recurrent/) simply says 

> if return_seq: 3-D Tensor [samples, timesteps, output dim]. else: 2-D Tensor Layer [samples, output dim].

I haven't tested with single directional RNN but since bidirectional and normal single directional use rnn_template under the covers I'd wager that they both behave in this way.

issue_title: Permanent plugin installation with certbot-auto
issue_body:
## My operating system is (include version):
Ubuntu 16.04.1 LTS

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):
certbot-auto version 0.22.2


## I ran this command and it produced this output:
```bash
$ certbot-auto renew
Upgrading certbot-auto 0.22.1 to 0.22.2...
Replacing certbot-auto...
Creating virtual environment...
Installing Python packages...
Installation succeeded.

[..]
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Processing /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/some-wildcard-certificate.conf
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Renewal configuration file /etc/letsencrypt/renewal/some-wildcard-certificate.conf (cert: some-wildcard-certificate) produced an unexpected error: 'Namespace' object has no attribute 'dns_cloudflare_credentials'. Skipping.
[..]
```

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:
The plugin `certbot-dns-cloudflare` was installed via `/opt/eff.org/certbot/venv/bin/pip install certbot-dns-cloudflare` at version 0.22.1. Our nightly certificate renewal process via `certbot-auto renew` updated certbot and purged the installed plugin. As it seems this is [intended behavior](https://community.letsencrypt.org/t/example-of-using-certbot-auto-with-route-53-dns/53021/8) but I expect a mission critical plugin like that to be installed permanently.

issue_title: Replace youckan by the builtin auth

Youckan is present for historical reasons, when Udata had no builtin auth system and multiple Etalab apps had to connect to an SSO.

These apps were removed and since then udata has a builtin login system with SSO capabilities.

issue_title: ManualParameter calls validate twice
issue_body:
The qcodes `ManualParameter` calls validate twice for a single set.
``` python
import qcodes
from qcodes.utils.validators import Validator

class MyValidator(Validator):

    def __init__(self, min_value=-float("inf"), max_value=float("inf")):
        pass

    def validate(self, value, context=''):
        print('validate!')

    is_numeric=True

p = qcodes.ManualParameter('p', initial_value=0, vals=MyValidator())
p.set(10)
```
The output is:
```
validate!
validate!
```
The validator should only be called once.

@WilliamHPNielsen @jenshnielsen

issue_title: KeyError on (supposedly) unknown tokens during numericalize
issue_body:
Hi guys,

We've been working on refactoring [OpenNMT/OpenNMT-py project](https://github.com/OpenNMT/OpenNMT-py) using our fork [Ubiqus/OpenNMT-py project](https://github.com/Ubiqus/OpenNMT-py). It includes distributed processing, being compatible with pytorch==0.4, and, what matters most in this case (I guess), is that we switched from epoch to step based training. 

Before that we was training a full epoch THEN comes validation THEN saving the checkpoint.

Now, we just counts iterations and validate/save depending on a parameter (each `-valid_steps` and `-save_checkpoint_steps` respectively).

While testing on toy sets (using: <https://github.com/Ubiqus/OpenNMT-py/blob/master/onmt/tests/test_models.sh>) we faced `KeyError` in vocabularies (`.itos` list). the key (word) itself isn't important, it's not every time the same. It may be lower case or not. More importantly, we suppose that it's only words that are unkown. In both case it happens when we start an iterator over the dataset
## Setup
* Using [Ubiqus/OpenNMT-py (branch: `vocab_error`)](https://github.com/Ubiqus/OpenNMT-py/commit/252830933639f8e9a88cf201c29392e2dd9d060b)
* Using `torchtext=0.2.1`

## Error during validation
Toy transformer model (it happens in other models as well) with `-valid_steps 1000`

#### Trace
```
Step 1000,  2000; acc:  46.32; ppl:  10.78; xent:   2.38; lr: 0.00395; 50260 / 50342 tok/s;     66 sec
Loading valid dataset from ./data/data.valid.1.pt, number of examples: 2819
Traceback (most recent call last):

    main(opt)
  File "train.py", line 28, in main
    single_main(opt)
  File "/home/moses/pytorchwork/AAN/U-NMT-bug/train_single.py", line 120, in main
    opt.valid_steps)
  File "/home/moses/pytorchwork/AAN/U-NMT-bug/onmt/trainer.py", line 176, in train
    valid_stats = self.validate(valid_iter)
  File "/home/moses/pytorchwork/AAN/U-NMT-bug/onmt/trainer.py", line 208, in validate
    for batch in valid_iter:
  File "/home/moses/pytorchwork/AAN/U-NMT-bug/onmt/inputters/inputter.py", line 423, in _iter_
    for batch in self.cur_iter:
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/torchtext/data/iterator.py", line 151, in _iter_
    self.train)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/torchtext/data/batch.py", line 27, in _init_
    setattr(self, name, field.process(batch, device=device, train=train))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/torchtext/data/field.py", line 188, in process
    tensor = self.numericalize(padded, device=device, train=train)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/torchtext/data/field.py", line 287, in numericalize
    arr = [[self.vocab.stoi[x] for x in ex] for ex in arr]
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/torchtext/data/field.py", line 287, in <listcomp>
    arr = [[self.vocab.stoi[x] for x in ex] for ex in arr]
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/dist-packages/torchtext/data/field.py", line 287, in <listcomp>
    arr = [[self.vocab.stoi[x] for x in ex] for ex in arr]
KeyError: 'Anand'
```
#### How to replicate 
(using [`test_models.sh`](https://github.com/Ubiqus/OpenNMT-py/blob/252830933639f8e9a88cf201c29392e2dd9d060b/onmt/tests/test_models.sh))
```
./onmt/tests/test_models.sh set_gpu set_debug transformer
```

## Error after saving

#### Trace
```
Start training...
Loading train dataset from ./data/data.train.1.pt, number of examples: 10000
Step 50,  2000; acc:  42.07; ppl:  22.44; xent:   3.11; lr: 0.00625; 54535 / 58156 tok/s;      3 sec
Saving checkpoint /tmp/onmt_tmp_model_step_50.pt
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "train.py", line 41, in <module>
    main(opt)
  File "train.py", line 28, in main
    single_main(opt)
  File "/home/pltrdy/pytorchwork/unmt/train_single.py", line 120, in main
    opt.valid_steps)
  File "/home/pltrdy/pytorchwork/unmt/onmt/trainer.py", line 141, in train
    for i, batch in enumerate(train_iter):
  File "/home/pltrdy/pytorchwork/unmt/onmt/inputters/inputter.py", line 423, in __iter__
    for batch in self.cur_iter:
  File "/home/pltrdy/anaconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/torchtext/data/iterator.py", line 180, in __iter__
    self.train)
  File "/home/pltrdy/anaconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/torchtext/data/batch.py", line 22, in __init__
    setattr(self, name, field.process(batch, device=device, train=train))
  File "/home/pltrdy/anaconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/torchtext/data/field.py", line 187, in process
    tensor = self.numericalize(padded, device=device, train=train)
  File "/home/pltrdy/anaconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/torchtext/data/field.py", line 286, in numericalize
    arr = [[self.vocab.stoi[x] for x in ex] for ex in arr]
  File "/home/pltrdy/anaconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/torchtext/data/field.py", line 286, in <listcomp>
    arr = [[self.vocab.stoi[x] for x in ex] for ex in arr]
  File "/home/pltrdy/anaconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/torchtext/data/field.py", line 286, in <listcomp>
    arr = [[self.vocab.stoi[x] for x in ex] for ex in arr]
KeyError: 'seafront'

```
#### How to replicate 
In `onmt/tests/test_models.sh` change, line 231 to `-save_checkpoint_steps 50 \` then run the same command:
```
./onmt/tests/test_models.sh set_gpu set_debug transformer
```
#### More about the saving.
We save the checkpoint with the vocabulary.  To do it we call `save_fields_to_vocab(self.fields)` ([model_saver.py#L111](https://github.com/Ubiqus/OpenNMT-py/blob/vocab_error/onmt/models/model_saver.py#L111)) which is ([inputter.py#L70](https://github.com/Ubiqus/OpenNMT-py/blob/vocab_error/onmt/inputters/inputter.py#L70)):
```
def save_fields_to_vocab(fields):
    vocab = []
    for k, f in fields.items():
        if f is not None and 'vocab' in f.__dict__:
            f.vocab.stoi = dict(f.vocab.stoi)
            vocab.append((k, f.vocab))
    return vocab
```
**NOTE**: the name of this function isn't relevant anymore.   
I'm not sure how this could lead to problems, but it may be related.


## Discussions
1. those exceptions may not be raised depending on `-valid_steps` and `-save_checkpoint_steps` values. Therefore the problem may occur because of a side-effect. For example, in the first example, the model is saved without error.
2. because of (1) this is not really critical. But we think that it may hides larger problems. 
3. During investigations, we found that the vocabularies are getting bigger and bigger during training (we checked `len(field.vocab.stoi)` that goes from 1000 to 50k+. New tokens are added and mapped to 0 (unk). We're not sure if this is wanted (and why). 
4. I don't think that the problem comes from torchtext, I'm just wondering if all of this makes sense to you and if you could suggest a way to fix it // better way to use torchtext.

Thanks a lot.
Paul

issue_title: Installation fails to find boost when boost is installed with MacPorts.

### Description
After wondering for a day or so why Facebook live streams were only giving me 360p, I finally discovered that even though I'd been installing new versions for several days (nightlies), my current version was stuck at 0.9.0

Quick uninstall and reinstall fixed the problem which I believe occurred because the Windows installer defaults to "Install for everyone on this machine" instead of the "Install only for me" which I previously used. issue_title: Add PAC (proxy auto-config) support (feature request)
issue_body:
### Checklist

- [ ] This is a bug report.
- [x] This is a feature request.
- [ ] This is a plugin (improvement) request.
- [ ] I have read the contribution guidelines.

### Description

At least 1/4 of supported websites use geo-restrictions to prevent users from undesirable countries to watch the content. Free reliable proxy servers are hard to find. Espesially located in small countries.

Some sites only restrict providing stream links, while streams themselves are accessible from around the world.

With PAC ( https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Proxy_auto-config ) file it is possible to use different scenarios, how given URLs or protocols are accessed. Thus, utilising proxies to find stream links only, would lower proxy servers load and make watching experience more comfortable with less disconnections, freezing and other difficulties.

There are a couple of Python projects relevant to the issue:
- PyPAC: http://pypac.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
- Pacparser: http://pacparser.manugarg.com/

issue_title: Bintray account for Streamlink has been rate-limited
issue_body:
> Hi Streamlink,
> 
> Your Bintray account is currently rate-limited due to overuse.
> 
> Customer Support should contact you at the earliest to remove the limits from your account. You may also contact them directly at support@jfrog.com.
> 
> Regards,
> The Bintray Team

I don't know whether the downloads have been abused or not (it doesn't let me see any download statistics on their website), but it looks like we've reached the traffic limit for this month already, and it's just the 9th of February today.

Maybe it's time thinking about a different release model that doesn't force Windows users to download nightlies from an external, auxiliary hoster, just because they want the latest version of a specific plugin. For now, we should add a small note to the download page to tell users to consider downloading a stable release instead of a nightly build, unless they've experienced a bug in the latest version.

As we've already discussed a few months ago (#811), using Github as a hoster for pre-releases could work, but only if we set up a different repo, so that the amount of notifications and spam can be kept down. I don't know though if we would be breaking Github's ToS with that.

Regarding the idea of letting Streamlink update its plugins automatically (#1347), I've already said that I don't think that we should turn Streamlink into a package manager of its own, as there are too many things which need to be taken into account and which also can go wrong. I also doubt that anybody would want to implement this kind of feature here.

My suggestion in order to reduce the number of nightly downloads, is publishing releases more often, in a 14-day schedule or so. This could even be automated, if desired. I don't actively use yt-dl, but they're releasing far more often and are using the [calendar versioning versioning scheme](https://calver.org/#youtube_dl). This lets people immediately know how old a release is and if we'd publish a new release every two weeks, the intention to download a nightly build gets reduced greatly.

issue_title: Ingest and stacker should enable compression on stored metadata documents
Consider relaxing the await-always-checkpoints rule to await-always-checkpoints-on-non-error
issue_body:
Currently, all the async functions in trio proper attempt to always issue a checkpoint on every call. The idea is that you want to be able to tell from reading your code where the checkpoints are, so this makes it more predictable – it's a static guarantee, so you don't have to reason carefully about unclear runtime conditions, and it's easy to explain to users.

However, I'm wondering if we might want to relax this slightly: maybe the rule should be, trio's async functions always checkpoint, *unless they raise an exception* (in which case there's no guarantee either way).

The reasoning here is that guaranteeing the checkpoint invariant for error paths turns out to be very tiresome and error-prone, both to implement and to test. Also, in some sense it's actually impossible to get this perfect – e.g. if you accidentally do `await trio.sleep(1,5)` then Python will raise `TypeError: sleep() takes 1 positional argument ...` before even invoking the function, so this call doesn't checkpoint.

Furthermore, we can guess that *usually* users aren't going to be caring about checkpoints for operations that raise exceptions... for example, it'd be unusual to have a loop where the only `await` was on an operation that always raised an exception that you caught and then looped around on. And the proposed rule is easy to explain, though not quite as easy as the original rule.

Another nice thing is the rule is still sufficiently deterministic that we could check it automatically, as suggested in #152.

issue_title: trio.socket should be more careful when re-exporting global functions
issue_body:
It looks like `getprotobyname`, `getservbyname`, `getservbyport` all do disk access and therefore I guess should be made async via `run_in_worker_thread`. (They're also [buggy](https://bugs.python.org/issue30482), but never mind...)

`gethostname` and `sethostname` appear to be direct syscalls (at least on linux). `ntohs` and friends and `inet_*` are pure computation, but `inet_ntoa`/`inet_aton` are considered deprecated in favor of `inet_ntop`/`inet_pton`. Maybe we shouldn't re-export them given this?

The `if_*` functions appear to involve some chatting with the kernel over netlink, but it's all on-CPU and finishes in well under a millisecond, so I don't think we need to treat it as blocking.

issue_title: Fix of java package name in exception message.
Thrift encoding breaks in python3
issue_body:
In python3, spans are unable to be encoded with thrift due to this line: https://github.com/jaegertracing/jaeger-client-python/blob/master/jaeger_client/thrift.py#L55

In python3, the `encode()` function returns a `bytes` literal, which then causes the following error in thrift, which is expecting a `str`:
```
...
  File "/Users/nziebart/.virtualenvs/nummus/lib/python3.6/site-packages/jaeger_client/thrift_gen/jaeger/ttypes.py", line 146, in write
    oprot.writeString(self.key)
  File "/Users/nziebart/.virtualenvs/nummus/lib/python3.6/site-packages/thrift/protocol/TProtocol.py", line 121, in writeString
    self.writeBinary(str_to_binary(str_val))
  File "/Users/nziebart/.virtualenvs/nummus/lib/python3.6/site-packages/thrift/compat.py", line 41, in str_to_binary
    return bytes(str_val, 'utf8')
TypeError: encoding without a string argument
```

It can be fixed by replacing the `_to_string` function linked above with:
```
        if six.PY2 and isinstance(s, unicode):
            return s.encode('utf8')
        else:
            return str(s)
```

I can open a PR for this tomorrow

issue_title: Introduce Opaque/Customized Operator
issue_body:
ONNX cannot cover all types of operators. But if users still want to represent their models using onnx, we probably should allow them to do so.

The proposal is adding experimental `opaque`/`customized` operators. So backend support is optional, and users can still represent their models in ONNX format, do the things they want, such as applying optimization.

issue_title: CV vs mean looks weird
issue_body:
I'm getting weird results from CV vs mean fitting (`vlm.score_cv_vs_mean()`). To reproduce the error, I first installed a fresh Anaconda:

```bash
bash Anaconda3-5.2.0-MacOSX-x86_64.sh 
```

Then I installed velocyto and its dependencies:

```bash
conda install -c r -c bioconda pysam
conda install numpy scipy cython numba matplotlib scikit-learn h5py click
pip install velocyto
```

I got a warning that "distributed 1.21.8 requires msgpack, which is not installed" so I did `pip install msgpack`to be sure. 

I then checked out the dentate gyrus notebook by doing `git checkout -- python/DentateGyrus.ipynb` and executed it one step at a time (including downloading the data from Kharchenko's server). After step 10, the expected result is

![unknown](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10656387/44830872-d6cf5e00-abd8-11e8-92b3-c08f59c4c54c.png)

but I got instead

![dentatecvmean](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10656387/44830880-e0f15c80-abd8-11e8-84bb-aa7f134ffd9b.png)

There's nothing apparently wrong with the data, because if I compute CV and mean manually I get:

![dentatecvmean_data](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10656387/44830898-01211b80-abd9-11e8-8ed8-ad96f4afdf2f.png)


Also tried using velocyto 0.17.8 but got the same weird result.

If I continue executing the dentate notebook, step 13 (PCA) goes to 100% on all cores and never seems to end (it used to be quick). 

I've run this notebook many times in the past with no issues. I also now tried the Haber notebook, and the CV-mean plot also looks weird. I have no idea what could be wrong, and would appreciate any suggestions.
